title: Latent Tire-State Features Via Simple State-Space Filtering
summary: Recent F1 tire-degradation work argues that latent tire pace with stint resets outperforms simpler autoregressive baselines.
rationale: Recent F1 tire-degradation work argues that latent tire pace with stint resets outperforms simpler autoregressive baselines. That suggests raw `TyreLife` and `Cumulative_Degradation` may be insufficient proxies for the hidden state that actually triggers pit calls.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint, model lap-time residuals after removing crude fuel/race-progress effects, then extract filtered latent level, slope, uncertainty, and post-pit reset magnitude from a lightweight Kalman/local-trend model. Add compound-specific latent-state features rather than replacing existing features.
expected_effect: Captures nonlinear wear, warmup, and noisy degradation regimes that trees may miss when using only observed aggregates.
risk: Observed columns are only weak proxies for full telemetry, so latent states may be noisy on wet sessions or rare compounds.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
